axis_responsibility: Support axis: checker profiles and module_registry are support evidence only; a profile grouping/label is a convenience selector, NOT a success/quality verdict and NOT a hidden target selector.
Agent axis: COO chooses which profile group to run; that choice is operator convenience, not authority.
Link axis: owns Movement; profile diet must not encode routing or gate decisions.
Human: owns success/quality judgment; green profiles remain evidence, never verdict.

returned.design_summary: Design BLOCKED on content access. The diet boundary the task asks for (separate/label heavy/provider/dogfood profiles from quick/core so COO runs targeted checks instead of --all) requires reading (a) the measurement profile-table to know per-profile weight/runtime, and (b) the profile YAML bodies + module_registry.yaml to know current grouping/labels and module fan-out. None of these bodies were readable in this session. A grounded design contract cannot be responsibly produced from filenames alone. Filename-level signals only SUGGEST candidate heavy/provider/dogfood profiles (e.g. adapter_usage_meter, building_automation, building_operator_driver0, coo_operating_chain, mcp_dispatch_wire, native_dispatch_brick_backstop, driver_public_intake_seal) vs likely-core (core, structure_template_integrity, tier_a_three_axis_conformance), but this is NOT content-verified and must not be acted on as-is.
edge_cases: A profile that is both quick AND provider/dogfood-dependent — labeling must not hide it from the full set.
module_registry references that would dangle if a profile is regrouped/renamed.
COO running a 'core' subset and wrongly treating subset-green as full-green (must stay clearly distinguished from --all).
Profiles requiring live provider/network (e.g. adapter/mcp/dogfood) must be isolable so quick runs do not silently require provider access — but cannot confirm which without reading bodies.
